description: Create comprehensive visualization of isoform architecture showing MTS extension and updated verdict
code:
import matplotlib
matplotlib.use('Agg')
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# The MTS extension from CG4389-PA (Q9V397)
mts_ext = "MSATRFLSAVGQISRQQLLQNKCTRALPISAQLLQRRRL"
print(f"=== MTS Extension Analysis (39 aa) ===")
print(f"Sequence: {mts_ext}")
print(f"Length: {len(mts_ext)} aa")

from collections import Counter
counts = Counter(mts_ext)
print(f"\nAmino acid composition:")
pos = counts.get('R', 0) + counts.get('K', 0)
neg = counts.get('D', 0) + counts.get('E', 0)
print(f"  R: {counts.get('R', 0)}, K: {counts.get('K', 0)} → total positive: {pos}")
print(f"  D: {counts.get('D', 0)}, E: {counts.get('E', 0)} → total negative: {neg}")
print(f"  Net charge: +{pos - neg}")
print(f"  Hydrophobic (LFIVA): {sum(counts.get(aa,0) for aa in 'LFIVA')}/39 ({sum(counts.get(aa,0) for aa in 'LFIVA')/39*100:.0f}%)")
print(f"  Ser/Thr (hydroxyl): {counts.get('S',0) + counts.get('T',0)}")

# Create comprehensive figure showing the isoform architecture
fig, axes = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(16, 11))

# Panel A: Isoform architecture
ax1 = axes[0]
ax1.set_xlim(-50, 820)
ax1.set_ylim(-0.5, 3.5)

# CG4389-PA (Q9V397, 783 aa)
y_pa = 3
ax1.barh(y_pa, 783, left=0, height=0.4, color='lightgray', edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
ax1.barh(y_pa, 39, left=0, height=0.4, color='#e74c3c', alpha=0.8)  # MTS
ax1.barh(y_pa, 3, left=780, height=0.4, color='#2ecc71', alpha=0.8)  # PTS1
ax1.text(-45, y_pa, 'CG4389-PA\n(Q9V397)\n783 aa', ha='right', va='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')
ax1.text(19, y_pa, 'MTS\n(39 aa)', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, color='white', fontweight='bold')
ax1.text(780, y_pa-0.35, 'SKL', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='#2ecc71', fontweight='bold')
ax1.annotate('DUAL:\nMito + Perox', xy=(783, y_pa), xytext=(810, y_pa),
            fontsize=8, color='#8e44ad', fontweight='bold',
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#8e44ad'))

# CG4389-PB (Q8IPE8, 744 aa)
y_pb = 2
ax1.barh(y_pb, 744, left=39, height=0.4, color='lightgray', edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
ax1.barh(y_pb, 3, left=780, height=0.4, color='#2ecc71', alpha=0.8)  # PTS1
ax1.text(-45, y_pb, 'CG4389-PB\n(Q8IPE8)\n744 aa', ha='right', va='center', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold',
         bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='yellow', alpha=0.3))
ax1.text(780, y_pb-0.35, 'SKL', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='#2ecc71', fontweight='bold')
ax1.annotate('PEROXISOME\nONLY', xy=(783, y_pb), xytext=(810, y_pb),
            fontsize=8, color='#2ecc71', fontweight='bold',
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#2ecc71'))

# Human HADHA for comparison
y_hadha = 1
ax1.barh(y_hadha, 763, left=0, height=0.4, color='lightgray', edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
ax1.barh(y_hadha, 36, left=0, height=0.4, color='#e74c3c', alpha=0.8)  # MTS
ax1.text(-45, y_hadha, 'H.sapiens\nHADHA\n763 aa', ha='right', va='center', fontsize=9)
ax1.text(18, y_hadha, 'MTS', ha='center', va='center', fontsize=8, color='white', fontweight='bold')
ax1.text(760, y_hadha-0.35, 'FYQ', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='#e74c3c')
ax1.annotate('MITO ONLY\n(no PTS1)', xy=(763, y_hadha), xytext=(810, y_hadha),
            fontsize=8, color='#e74c3c', fontweight='bold',
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#e74c3c'))

# Human EHHADH
y_ehh = 0
ax1.barh(y_ehh, 723, left=0, height=0.4, color='lightgray', edgecolor='black', linewidth=1)
ax1.barh(y_ehh, 3, left=720, height=0.4, color='#2ecc71', alpha=0.8)  # PTS1
ax1.text(-45, y_ehh, 'H.sapiens\nEHHADH\n723 aa', ha='right', va='center', fontsize=9)
ax1.text(720, y_ehh-0.35, 'SKL', ha='center', fontsize=8, color='#2ecc71', fontweight='bold')
ax1.annotate('PEROXISOME\nONLY', xy=(723, y_ehh), xytext=(810, y_ehh),
            fontsize=8, color='#2ecc71', fontweight='bold',
            arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#2ecc71'))

# Add domains for PB
for y_pos in [y_pa, y_pb]:
    offset = 39 if y_pos == y_pa else 39
    # ECH domain
    ax1.barh(y_pos, 280, left=offset+10, height=0.15, color='steelblue', alpha=0.5)
    # 3HCDH domains
    ax1.barh(y_pos, 280, left=offset+370, height=0.15, color='coral', alpha=0.5)

ax1.set_xlabel('Position (aa)')
ax1.set_yticks([])
ax1.set_title('A. Isoform Architecture: CG4389/Mtpalpha vs Human Orthologs', fontweight='bold', fontsize=12)

# Legend
from matplotlib.patches import Patch
legend_elements = [Patch(facecolor='#e74c3c', alpha=0.8, label='MTS (mitochondrial targeting)'),
                   Patch(facecolor='#2ecc71', alpha=0.8, label='PTS1 (peroxisomal targeting)'),
                   Patch(facecolor='steelblue', alpha=0.5, label='Enoyl-CoA hydratase domain'),
                   Patch(facecolor='coral', alpha=0.5, label='3-hydroxyacyl-CoA DH domain')]
ax1.legend(handles=legend_elements, loc='upper right', fontsize=8)

# Panel B: MTS helical wheel projection (simplified)
ax2 = axes[1]
ax2.set_xlim(-2, 12)
ax2.set_ylim(-2, 3)

# Show MTS as amphipathic helix
# Eisenberg hydrophobicity for helical wheel
eisenberg = {
    'A': 0.62, 'R': -2.53, 'N': -0.78, 'D': -0.90, 'C': 0.29,
    'Q': -0.85, 'E': -0.74, 'G': 0.48, 'H': -0.40, 'I': 1.38,
    'L': 1.06, 'K': -1.50, 'M': 0.64, 'F': 1.19, 'P': 0.12,
    'S': -0.18, 'T': -0.05, 'W': 0.81, 'Y': 0.26, 'V': 1.08
}

# Display MTS sequence with coloring
ax2.text(0, 2.5, 'CG4389-PA MTS extension (39 aa):', fontsize=10, fontweight='bold')
for i, aa in enumerate(mts_ext):
    x = i * 0.27
    if aa in 'RK':
        color = '#3498db'  # positive = blue
    elif aa in 'DE':
        color = '#e74c3c'  # negative = red
    elif aa in 'LFIVA':
        color = '#f39c12'  # hydrophobic = orange
    else:
        color = '#95a5a6'  # other = gray
    ax2.text(x, 1.8, aa, fontsize=11, fontweight='bold', color=color, 
             fontfamily='monospace', ha='center')
    ax2.text(x, 1.3, str(i+1), fontsize=6, color='gray', ha='center')

ax2.text(0, 0.7, 'Color: ', fontsize=9)
ax2.text(1.2, 0.7, 'R/K (+charge)', fontsize=9, color='#3498db', fontweight='bold')
ax2.text(3.8, 0.7, 'L/F/I/V/A (hydrophobic)', fontsize=9, color='#f39c12', fontweight='bold')
ax2.text(7.5, 0.7, 'other', fontsize=9, color='#95a5a6')

ax2.text(0, 0.1, 'Properties: Net charge = +7  |  No acidic residues  |  38% hydrophobic', fontsize=10)
ax2.text(0, -0.4, 'MitoProtII: 97% MTS probability  |  TargetP: 85% MTS probability', fontsize=10, color='#e74c3c')
ax2.text(0, -0.9, 'MPP cleavage: Ends with RRRL → cleavage between R and L yields PB N-terminus (M...)', 
         fontsize=9, color='#8e44ad')
ax2.text(0, -1.4, 'PB starts at M39 → MSTNPAP... (= Q8IPE8)', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold', color='#2ecc71')

ax2.axis('off')
ax2.set_title('B. MTS Extension Properties', fontweight='bold', fontsize=12)

# Panel C: Updated verdict
ax3 = axes[2]
ax3.axis('off')
verdict_text = """UPDATED VERDICT: SUPPORTED

Q8IPE8 = CG4389-PB (the shorter isoform WITHOUT the MTS)

Evidence for peroxisomal localization:
✓ Q8IPE8 was DIRECTLY TESTED: mCherry-CG4389-PB colocalized
  with PMP34-Cerulean in S2 cells (Figure 4, PMID:22758915)
✓ Paper states PB/PC are "exclusively peroxisomal"
✓ Canonical PTS1 (SKL) conserved across Drosophila genus
✓ No mitochondrial targeting sequence in this isoform

Mitochondrial annotations likely reflect PA isoform (Q9V397):
• Q9V397 has 39-aa MTS extension (net charge +7, 97% MitoProtII)
• The HDA proteomics evidence cannot distinguish PA from PB
• Q9V397 is the isoform predicted to be dually localized

RECOMMENDATION: RETAIN GO:0005777. Consider upgrading to
GO:0005782 (peroxisomal matrix) since PTS1 targets to matrix.
"""
ax3.text(0.02, 0.95, verdict_text, transform=ax3.transAxes,
         fontsize=10, verticalalignment='top', fontfamily='monospace',
         bbox=dict(boxstyle='round', facecolor='#d5f5e3', alpha=0.8))
ax3.set_title('C. Updated Curation Assessment', fontweight='bold', fontsize=12)

plt.tight_layout()
plt.savefig('isoform_analysis.png', dpi=150, bbox_inches='tight')
plt.show()
print("Isoform analysis figure saved")
